narrative: Your loan application was carefully reviewed by our credit model, which looks at various aspects of your financial profile to assess risk, drawing from patterns in historical data from past borrowers in Germany. With your request for 7721 Deutsche Marks over 24 months to purchase furniture or equipment, and given your current checking account status showing less than 0 DM available, the model calculated a 43.5% probability that this could lead to challenges in repayment—high enough to classify you as a bad credit risk and deny the application. The decision hinged on just a few key factors from your situation that stood out the most. First, your savings status, listed as unknown or none, actually worked in your favor more than you might think; it pulled the prediction toward a lower risk compared to many approved applicants who also had limited savings, suggesting this alone wasn't a dealbreaker. However, your checking account being in the red outweighed that positive note, strongly pushing the model toward seeing higher risk, especially since approved borrowers more often had no account issues or positive balances. Adding to the concern was the relatively large loan amount you requested—more than double the average for those who were approved—which signaled potential strain even with your low installment rate of just 1% of disposable income and steady job as a skilled worker. While your credit history of existing paid accounts is common among approvals and your short employment duration under a year aligns with some successful cases, these didn't offset the top concerns enough. Compared to typical approved applicants, who often seek smaller amounts around 3200 DM with longer average credit durations of about 20 months and more stable account statuses, your profile appeared riskier in those critical areas. It's understandable to feel disappointed, especially as a 25-year-old male living independently in owned housing with no other debts or guarantors pulling you down. To improve your chances next time, consider building up your checking account balance, reducing the requested amount, or providing more details on savings to shift those key factors positively. You might also wait a bit longer in your current employment or residence—averaging nearly 3 years for approvals—to demonstrate stability. We encourage you to reapply once you've addressed these, or explore smaller credit options to build a stronger history. Your situation shows promise in areas like having no other installment plans and just one dependent, so targeted improvements could make a real difference.
